title: Kitchen degreaser spray
subtitle: A white vinegar and lemon essential oil spray for kitchen surfaces
excerpt: A kitchen surface cleaner made from white vinegar and lemon essential oil, diluted with distilled water. Effective on light cooking grease, food splashes, and general kitchen surface residue. Do not use on natural stone worktops. Fill into a 500 ml spray bottle.
sourceNotes: Dri-Pak white vinegar kitchen cleaning applications (dri-pak.co.uk, accessed May 2026). Lemon EO d-limonene content as a degreasing aid: Smith et al., Limonene as a green solvent (Chemical Society Reviews, 2018).

content.text: A kitchen surface spray made from white vinegar and lemon essential oil. The acetic acid in the vinegar cuts through light cooking grease and food residue. Lemon essential oil contains
, which adds mild solvent action and leaves a clean citrus scent. This spray is effective on laminate worktops, glass hob surrounds, stainless steel, ceramic tiles, and appliance exteriors.

such as marble, granite, or quartz composite: the acid etches the surface. Use a pH-neutral cleaner on stone. Also avoid using on cast iron, which the acid will pit, and on waxed wood surfaces, which the acid will strip.

items.name: White vinegar (cleaning grade)
items.prepNote: Cleaning-grade white vinegar (5% acidity). The primary cleaning and degreasing agent.

items.name: Distilled water
items.prepNote: Dilutes the vinegar. Distilled water avoids introducing new mineral deposits onto cleaned surfaces.

items.name: Lemon essential oil
items.prepNote: 1% of total spray volume. Add to the bottle and shake before each use as lemon EO will float on the liquid.

content.text: Pour the white vinegar and distilled water into the spray bottle using the funnel. Add the lemon essential oil. Seal and shake for 30 seconds. Label with the making date.

Shake the bottle before each use. Spray onto the surface and wipe with a clean cloth. For baked-on grease or dried food, spray generously and leave for 2 to 3 minutes before wiping. No rinsing is required on most surfaces, but wipe dry to prevent water marks on stainless steel.

Store in the spray bottle away from direct sunlight. Keeps for 12 months. The lemon essential oil scent will fade after 6 to 12 months. Shake before every use. Refill with the same proportions when the bottle runs low.
